target.title: feat(agents): inherit requester model for subagents
target.body:
## Summary

- Let native subagent spawns resolve `model: "inherit"` from the requester session's active model, including model context passed through `sessions_spawn` and persisted requester session state fallback.
- Preserve existing model precedence: explicit tool model, per-agent subagent model, default subagent model, target agent model, then global/runtime default.
- Keep model aliases authoritative, so a configured alias named `inherit` still selects that concrete model instead of the inheritance sentinel.
- Update subagent docs, config type comments, prompt snapshots, and focused regression coverage for native spawn, ACP startup model behavior, sessions patch hints, and model selection.
- Supersedes #87628 with a clean branch based on latest `main` and a single commit.

## Linked context

Closes N/A

Related #87628

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request known; this is a replacement for the previous contributor PR.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Native `sessions_spawn` can explicitly inherit the requester session's active model while preserving existing configured model precedence and alias handling.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, using the production agent model selection and subagent spawn planning modules through `node --import tsx`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```console
$ node --import tsx --eval 'const { resolveSubagentModelAndThinkingPlan } = await import("./src/agents/subagent-spawn-plan.ts"); const baseCfg = { session: { mainKey: "main", scope: "per-sender" } }; const cases = [ ["explicit inherit", resolveSubagentModelAndThinkingPlan({ cfg: baseCfg, targetAgentId: "research", modelOverride: "inherit", inheritedModel: "openai/gpt-5.4" })], ["configured inherit", resolveSubagentModelAndThinkingPlan({ cfg: { ...baseCfg, agents: { defaults: { subagents: { model: "inherit" } } } }, targetAgentId: "research", inheritedModel: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6" })], ["alias named inherit", resolveSubagentModelAndThinkingPlan({ cfg: { ...baseCfg, agents: { defaults: { model: { primary: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6" }, models: { "openai/gpt-5.4": { alias: "inherit" } }, subagents: { model: "inherit" } } } }, targetAgentId: "research", inheritedModel: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6" })], ["missing requester", resolveSubagentModelAndThinkingPlan({ cfg: baseCfg, targetAgentId: "research", modelOverride: "inherit" })] ]; for (const [name, plan] of cases) { const status = plan.status; const resolved = plan.resolvedModel; const patchModel = plan.initialSessionPatch?.model ?? ""; const message = plan.error ?? ""; console.log(`${name}: status=${status} resolved=${resolved} patchModel=${patchModel} message=${message}`); }'
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```console
explicit inherit: status=ok resolved=openai/gpt-5.4 patchModel=openai/gpt-5.4 message=
configured inherit: status=ok resolved=anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 patchModel=anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 message=
alias named inherit: status=ok resolved=openai/gpt-5.4 patchModel=openai/gpt-5.4 message=
missing requester: status=error resolved=inherit patchModel= message=Cannot inherit subagent model because the requester session has no active model. Pass an explicit model or configure a concrete agents.defaults.subagents.model value.
```

Observed result after fix: `model: "inherit"` resolves to the requester model for explicit and configured native subagent planning, aliases named `inherit` still resolve as concrete model aliases, and missing requester model context returns the actionable inheritance error.

What was not tested: Live provider generation was outside scope; this branch changes the child session model selection before provider calls.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm docs:list` was attempted for the docs touch, but pnpm tried to run `pnpm install` and failed in non-TTY mode with `ERR_PNPM_ABORTED_REMOVE_MODULES_DIR_NO_TTY`; relevant docs pages were read directly instead.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The previous PR branch needed replacement after merge/rebase churn; this branch reapplies the same feature on latest `main` as one clean commit.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --write`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/live-model-dynamic-candidates.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/live-model-dynamic-candidates.test.ts`
- Final post-commit proof: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/live-model-dynamic-candidates.test.ts src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Model selection tests for explicit/configured `inherit`, requester model resolution, missing requester model, and `inherit` aliases.
- Native `spawnSubagentDirect` and `sessions_spawn` tool tests proving requester model context is forwarded and persisted.
- ACP spawn regression coverage was rerun to keep target agent primary model startup behavior intact.
- Prompt snapshots were regenerated for updated tool descriptions.

What failed before this fix, if known?

During branch reconstruction, `src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts` caught a merge bug where ACP startup runtime options stopped using the target agent primary model. The helper default was restored and the ACP/model tests passed afterward.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A; focused regression tests and snapshots were added or updated.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Native subagent model selection can now explicitly inherit the requester session model.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `agents.defaults.subagents.model` and `agents.list[].subagents.model` now document and accept the `"inherit"` sentinel while preserving aliases named `inherit`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No direct security/auth/secret/network changes. Tool execution behavior changes only for native subagent model selection when `inherit` is selected.

What is the highest-risk area?

Model precedence across native subagents, ACP runtime options, and alias resolution.

How is that risk mitigated?

Focused tests cover explicit overrides, configured defaults, alias precedence, missing requester model errors, native persistence, ACP startup model preservation, and sessions patch behavior.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

CI and maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Author work is complete locally; live provider generation was outside scope and may be requested by maintainers if needed.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

No new reviewer comments on this replacement PR yet. It replaces #87628 after branch churn.
target.author: gausons
